institution.name: UC San Diego Health
aliases: UC San Diego School of Medicine — Visiting Fourth-Year Students program (covering UCSD Health placement)

searchTermsTried: UC San Diego School of Medicine visiting medical student VSLO elective international site:ucsd.edu

rejectedPages.title: Visiting Elective Scholarship Program for Department of Medicine Clerkships

candidateFindings.shortQuote: We accept applications from students from North American (US, Canada, Puerto Rico) LCME-accredited medical schools and COCA-accredited osteopathic schools.
candidateFindings.specialtyDecision: Multispecialty via VSLO catalog; Rady Children's Hospital named as a rotation site

candidateFindings.shortQuote: We do not accept applications from students who attend international or off-shore medical schools for this program.
Only fourth-year (senior) medical students may apply for clinical electives at UC San Diego School of Medicine.

negativeEvidence.strongNegativeEvidence: UCSD has the most direct international-exclusion language found so far in P101: 'We do not accept applications from students who attend international or off-shore medical schools for this program.' Eligibility limited to North American LCME and COCA schools.
negativeEvidence.weakNegativeEvidence: Cost, visa, and duration absent from source; UCSD Health hospital not separately named as placement (only Rady Children's mentioned).

nextAction: For public-safe activation: (a) SCHOOL_LEVEL_SOURCE — UCSD SOM hub; UCSD Health hospital not separately named (only Rady Children's mentioned); (b) NA LCME + COCA only, fourth-year only; (c) international + off-shore EXPLICITLY EXCLUDED — no IMG-friendly claim permitted; (d) cost/visa/duration NOT_STATED on source; (e) VSLO required, applications March 1 / offers April 1.
plainEnglishSummary: UCSD School of Medicine's visiting students program accepts only fourth-year students from North American LCME or COCA-accredited schools. International and off-shore medical schools are explicitly excluded — the most direct exclusion language found in P101 so far. Applications via VSLO with March 1 open / April 1 offers. Fee, duration, and visa not stated on the hub.
driftCheck: This advanced national USCE discovery because California gained a third classified institution with the clearest international-exclusion language captured to date; this verbatim quote will protect future cards (no IMG-friendly claim permitted on UCSD).
